entries.title: Daily drill: GCP Cloud Bigtable Timestamp-Prefix Row Key Hotspot — Sequential Write Concentration Saturates Tablet Servers
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty database hotfix drill: a GCP Cloud Bigtable feature store for 3 ML recommendation models experiences 20× SLO breach (P99 4,210ms vs 200ms target) when a marketing campaign 2.1× write-rate increase pushes 2 of 6 tablet servers to 96% CPU. Root cause is the classic timestamp-prefix anti-pattern — monotonically increasing keys concentrate 100% of current writes on the same lexicographic range. Exercise covers Key Visualizer hotspot triage, Dataflow throttle as immediate mitigation, reversed-timestamp key redesign for balanced distribution with per-user recency ordering, and zero-downtime dual-write migration to the new table.
items_added: GCP Cloud Bigtable Timestamp-Prefix Row Key Hotspot — Sequential Write Concentration Saturates Tablet Servers, Feature Store P99 Latency 20× SLO

entries.title: Daily drill: Ray Serve Head Node Preemption — Stale GCS Actor Handles Zombie All GPU Workers
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill: Azure AKS spot VM preemption kills the Ray head node, causing GCS epoch rollover. All 8 GPU worker replicas (Mistral-7B on A100-40GB) become zombie actors holding 36.4 GB GPU memory each with 0% utilization, blocking all 14,200 active WebSocket sessions for 22 minutes. Exercise covers epoch-mismatch triage via ray status, immediate mitigation via daemonset rollout restart, and long-term fixes including Redis-backed GCS persistence and IMDS eviction alerting.
items_added: Ray Serve Head Node Preemption — Stale GCS Actor Handles Zombie All GPU Workers

entries.title: Daily drill: Kafka Consumer Livelock — max.poll.interval.ms Breach During Downstream Throttle
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Java Kafka consumer group on AWS EKS enters an infinite livelock when a SendGrid API slowdown (48ms → 11.2s) causes batch processing to exceed max.poll.interval.ms=300s. All 6 consumers repeatedly get ejected and rejoin without committing offsets, growing lag at 195K messages/minute across 120 partitions. Exercise covers triage via JMX rebalance-rate metrics, immediate mitigation by reducing max.poll.records, circuit-breaker isolation of the downstream, and prevention via dynamic poll sizing and rebalance-rate alerting.
items_added: Kafka Consumer Livelock — max.poll.interval.ms Breach During Downstream Throttle

entries.title: Daily drill: MySQL InnoDB Gap Lock Deadlock Storm — Inventory Reservation Cascade During Flash Sale
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty MySQL 8.0 hotfix drill: a Java Spring Boot inventory service hits a 34.7% error rate when InnoDB gap locks on an out-of-stock range query deadlock with Insert Intention Locks from concurrent order inserts during a flash sale (8,400 concurrent writes). entries.title: Daily drill: Apache Flink RocksDB Checkpoint Timeout — L0 Compaction I/O Starvation on Feature Pipeline
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed systems hotfix drill: a Flink 1.18.1 streaming feature pipeline (32 TMs on GKE, RocksDB state backend) experiences 3 consecutive checkpoint failures when an upstream Pub/Sub publisher quadruples event batch size (128KB→512KB), triggering simultaneous L0 compaction across all 32 TaskManagers and saturating PD-SSD write bandwidth at 1.78 GB/s. Flink restarts from a 13-minute-old checkpoint, serving stale features to 14 ML ranking models for 14 minutes. Exercise covers RocksDB L0 stall triage via Flink Prometheus metrics, compaction thread throttle as immediate mitigation, upstream batch size reversion, local NVMe migration, and managed memory configuration.
items_added: Apache Flink RocksDB Checkpoint Timeout — L0 Compaction I/O Starvation on Feature Pipeline

entries.title: Daily drill: vLLM Speculative Decoding Draft Model Vocab Mismatch — Corrupted Responses After Target Model Rollout
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill on GKE: vLLM 0.4.2 speculative decoding returns HTTP 200 but only 7–9 token truncated responses after a target model upgrade expands the tokenizer vocab from 32K to 36K while the draft model remains at 32K. A vLLM engine off-by-one bug in the spec-decode rejection rollback loop corrupts the output buffer pointer on last-token rejections. Exercise covers spec-decode metric triage (acceptance_rate→0.00), disabling spec-decode as immediate mitigation, upgrading to vLLM 0.4.3, and adding ArgoCD PreSync vocab-parity hooks and synthetic quality canaries.
items_added: vLLM Speculative Decoding Draft Model Vocab Mismatch — Corrupted Responses After Target Model Rollout

Exercise covers structured concurrency fix via JoinSet, SIGTERM drain guard, terminationGracePeriodSeconds tuning, and CI enforcement via Pub/Sub emulator integration tests.

entries.title: Daily drill: Elasticsearch Disk Flood Stage — force_merge Segment Merge Overhead Triggers Mass Index Read-Only Lockout
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: an automated force_merge on 280GB of aging Elasticsearch indices generates temporary merge-overhead disk usage that, combined with a G1GC Full GC pause deferring segment release, crosses the 95% flood-stage watermark and applies read_only_allow_delete=true to all indices cluster-wide. Standard cluster health shows GREEN throughout; 847 writes/sec return HTTP 403 for 6 minutes with 304,920 events dropped before the on-call discovers the cause. Exercise covers index-block triage via settings API (invisible to standard health checks), immediate block-clear, safe force_merge strategy, and automated flood-stage remediation Lambda.
items_added: Elasticsearch Disk Flood Stage — force_merge Segment Merge Overhead Triggers Mass Index Read-Only Lockout

entries.title: Daily drill: SQS Visibility Timeout Race — Lambda Duration Spike Causes Duplicate Payment Charges During Flash Sale
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: AWS Lambda Node.js payment processor on SQS has its Duration P99 pushed from 17s to 31s when Stripe degrades to 1.2s and a SageMaker fraud endpoint saturates at 9.8s P99 during a flash sale — exceeding the 30s SQS VisibilityTimeout. Combined with an idempotency ordering bug (Stripe called before the DynamoDB conditional write), 847 customers are double-charged ($158,389 total). Exercise covers X-Ray dependency triage, VisibilityTimeout increase, Lambda concurrency throttle, idempotency-first reordering with DynamoDB PutItem as a distributed mutex, and Stripe native idempotency keys as defense-in-depth.
items_added: SQS Visibility Timeout Race — Lambda Duration Spike Causes Duplicate Payment Charges During Flash Sale

entries.title: Daily drill: Vault Dynamic Secret max_ttl Reduction — Synchronized Lease Expiry Causes Mass PostgreSQL Auth Failure
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty infrastructure hotfix drill: a Vault operator shortens the PostgreSQL secrets engine max_ttl from 24h to 1h on production roles, causing all 96 Go service pods' vault-agent leases to expire simultaneously 1 hour later. The Go pgx v5 pool is static (initialized once at startup) and doesn't reinitialize when vault-agent writes new credentials, leaving all 12 services unable to connect to RDS PostgreSQL for 22 minutes. Exercise covers credential-vs-network triage, rolling restart mitigation, fsnotify-based hot credential rotation fix, and Vault lease staggering prevention.
items_added: Vault Dynamic Secret max_ttl Reduction — Synchronized Lease Expiry Causes Mass PostgreSQL Auth Failure

entries.title: Daily drill: Azure AKS CoreDNS ndots:5 Storm — DNS Search Domain Amplification Kills Kafka and Service Mesh Connectivity
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty infrastructure hotfix drill: on Azure AKS, Kubernetes ndots:5 default causes 180 Java pods to generate 28,512 upstream DNS queries/sec (28× the Azure Private DNS Resolver quota of 1,000 QPS) after a KEDA scale-out for a product launch, killing Kafka connectivity and exhausting HikariCP database connection pools. Covers FQDN trailing-dot immediate fix, ndots:1 dnsConfig as durable solution, CoreDNS NXDOMAIN alerting, and NodeLocal DNSCache for long-term DNS throughput scaling.
items_added: Azure AKS CoreDNS ndots:5 Storm — DNS Search Domain Amplification Kills Kafka and Service Mesh Connectivity

entries.title: Daily drill: RabbitMQ Quorum Queue Memory Alarm — Celery Prefetch Surge OOM Wave Blocks All Pika Producers on GKE
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Celery `worker_prefetch_multiplier` bump from 100→2000 causes 4 of 24 GKE worker pods to OOMKill, returning 8,000 unacked messages to RabbitMQ and triggering a memory alarm (41.8% of 64 GB) that blocks all 14 Pika producer connections. The coordinator's `wait_for_confirms_or_die(timeout=5.0)` treats the transient block as a fatal error, causing 100% error rate for 17 minutes; a misrouted liveness probe cascades into a coordinator pod restart storm. Exercise covers memory-alarm triage via `rabbitmqctl`, Celery scale-to-zero as immediate relief, `CELERYD_PREFETCH_MULTIPLIER=1` for long-running tasks, Pika `Connection.Blocked` callback handling, lazy queue mode to decouple depth from RAM, and prefetch guard at container startup.
items_added: RabbitMQ Quorum Queue Memory Alarm — Celery worker_prefetch_multiplier Surge Triggers OOM Wave and Blocks All Pika Producers on GKE

entries.title: Daily drill: Temporal Workflow Sticky Queue Drain Stall — ML Training Pipeline Timeout Cascade During Worker Rolling Restart
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a dormant Go SDK misconfiguration (StickyScheduleToStartTimeout=0 maps to infinite wait, not 'disabled') causes 92 Temporal TrainModelWorkflow instances to hang on terminated-pod sticky queues during a GKE 1.28→1.29 rolling restart. Workflows time out after 5 minutes, while their orphaned A100-80GB training activities continue burning $32.77/hr each — accumulating $17,499 in orphaned GPU compute before the on-call terminates them. Exercise covers sticky queue triage via tctl, orphaned GPU termination, StickyScheduleToStartTimeout fix (set to 10s or omit for server default), bulk workflow reset, and a pre-rolling-restart queue-depth gate in CI/CD.
items_added: Temporal Workflow Sticky Queue Drain Stall — ML Training Pipeline Timeout Cascade During Worker Rolling Restart

entries.title: Daily drill: TGI Streaming Zombie Sessions — iOS Background TCP Suspension Exhausts KV Cache Pool
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill: iOS 4.2.0 ships a background-fetch feature that suspends TCP sockets without sending FIN, causing TGI 2.1.0 Rust streaming tasks to block indefinitely on poll_write (EAGAIN). Over 4h 14min, 2,847 zombie SSE sessions accumulate and exhaust the entire 8.5 GB A10G KV cache pool on SageMaker, producing 89.3% error rate with near-zero GPU utilization. Exercise covers zombie-session triage via GPU memory vs utilization disparity, SageMaker endpoint cycling as immediate mitigation, TGI 2.2.0 --client-idle-timeout fix, iOS app TCP FIN remediation, and a compound CloudWatch alarm targeting the unique high-VRAM/low-compute fingerprint.
